methodsAttempted.query: site:sggw.edu.pl (AI OR ChatGPT OR "generative AI" OR "artificial intelligence" OR "sztuczna inteligencja") (policy OR guidelines OR regulation OR academic integrity OR teaching OR assessment OR privacy) "Warsaw University of Life Sciences"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official English Rector Ordinance No. 93 PDF, the official English thesis page, research-output pages, study-offer material, and generic AI news/tag pages.
Firecrawl search found the official Polish thesis page and Polish Rector Ordinance No. 93 PDF, plus lower-specificity faculty and library seminar pages.
Firecrawl search found the official Polish Circular Letter No. 9 PDF about marking text fragments and objects generated by generative AI tools.
Robots.txt was fetched and allows the public thesis pages and PDF upload paths used here; only /wp-admin/ is disallowed.

artifacts.summary: Official discovery found a central Polish SGGW thesis page and public Rector documents governing generative AI use by persons undergoing education: Rector Ordinance No. 93 and Circular Letter No. 9. English official versions were also found and treated as helper/duplicate context. Research-output, study-offer, news, and faculty pages were rejected for primary policy claims.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Polish SGGW thesis page lists the GenAI section and links Rector Ordinance No. 93 and Circular Letter No. 9.

stopConditions: Stop after public central Polish SGGW Rector documents provide narrow AI-use, disclosure, academic-integrity, teaching, and data-handling evidence.
Do not infer university-wide policy from faculty pages, research metadata, event announcements, program brochures, or English translations when Polish originals are available.

artifacts.claimText: SGGW publishes Rector Ordinance No. 93 on the principles for using generative artificial intelligence tools in the works of persons undergoing education at the university.

citation.citationTitle: Zarządzenie Nr 93 Rektora SGGW z dnia 30 września 2024 r.
citation.publisher: Szkoła Główna Gospodarstwa Wiejskiego w Warszawie

artifacts.claimText: SGGW says a learner may not submit personal data, legally protected secrets, third-party copyrighted works, or SGGW commercialization-related creative-result data to publicly available generative AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Zarządzenie Nr 93 Rektora SGGW z dnia 30 września 2024 r.
citation.publisher: Szkoła Główna Gospodarstwa Wiejskiego w Warszawie

artifacts.claimText: SGGW requires course tutors to establish and communicate rules for using generative AI tools in courses, including interim and final work used to verify learning outcomes.

citation.citationTitle: Zarządzenie Nr 93 Rektora SGGW z dnia 30 września 2024 r.
citation.publisher: Szkoła Główna Gospodarstwa Wiejskiego w Warszawie

artifacts.claimText: SGGW requires learners using generative AI in their work to openly indicate the form and scope of use and mark generated text fragments or objects; content generated with AI but not indicated is not recognized as content created by the learner.

citation.citationTitle: Zarządzenie Nr 93 Rektora SGGW z dnia 30 września 2024 r.
citation.publisher: Szkoła Główna Gospodarstwa Wiejskiego w Warszawie

artifacts.claimText: SGGW Circular Letter No. 9 says unmodified generative-AI output included in work should be quoted or otherwise distinguished and given a note identifying the tool; the work should also include a separate literature-list section for generative AI tools used.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim based on the official Polish Rector ordinance title.
Approved as a narrow privacy/data-handling claim limited to publicly available generative AI tools and the categories named in § 3 ust. 2.
Approved as a teaching/assessment claim because it follows the ordinance language about course tutors and work used to verify learning outcomes.
Approved as an academic-integrity claim. The claim does not add sanctions beyond the source language and avoids broader misconduct conclusions.
Approved as a narrow marking/citation-method claim based on Circular Letter No. 9; detailed examples were not converted into separate mandatory claims.

artifacts.title: Warsaw University of Life Sciences SGGW generative AI rules for student work
artifacts.summary: Staged candidate claims for SGGW are based on official Polish Rector documents: Ordinance No. 93 on generative AI use in works by persons undergoing education and Circular Letter No. 9 on marking AI-generated text and objects. The strongest evidence covers source status, public GenAI data restrictions, course-level communication duties, learner disclosure duties, and marking/listing methods for generative AI tools.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are limited to public SGGW central sources found during this crawl and do not infer faculty-specific, course-specific, or unpublished rules.
English official PDFs were found but treated as helper translations because Polish originals were available.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
